description: Universal persona materials baseline — capability classification per 4+5 architecture. This is NOT a JSON Schema validator. It declares which capabilities are required/optional/dormant for a standard OpenPersona persona (P11-grade baseline).

soul.description: Identity, ethics, and personality — who the persona IS

body.description: Substrate of existence — how the persona lives in the world

faculty.description: Persistent capabilities that shape how the persona perceives or expresses

expression.description: How the persona communicates with the world
expression.note: Text output is baseline — all LLMs provide this; no faculty declaration needed. Voice and avatar are optional enhancements.

avatar.author: neiljo-gy
avatar.securityNote: VirusTotal Suspicious (expected — skill executes npx avatar-runtime at runtime, pulling remote npm code). OpenClaw Benign medium confidence. First-party trust overrides scan flag; verify GitHub repo acnlabs/avatar-runtime and pin package version before production use.
avatar.note: Provides Live2D / VRM rendering for personas with body.appearance declared. See _meta.firstPartyEcosystem.avatar-runtime.

cognition.description: How the persona thinks and remembers across time

ontology.author: oswalpalash
ontology.note: Typed knowledge graph (Person/Project/Task/Event/Document etc.) stored locally in memory/ontology/graph.jsonl. Python script, no network calls, no credentials. Enhances memory faculty with structured, queryable, relational knowledge beyond flat memories.jsonl.

self-improving.author: ivangdavila
self-improving.note: HOT/WARM/COLD tiered memory with automatic promotion/demotion rules. Instruction-only, cross-platform, no credentials. Adoption pattern: soft-ref; persona reads ~/self-improving/memory.md at conversation start and applies patterns within Soul-layer constraints (immutableTraits, boundaries). Write path stays in the skill's native storage; OpenPersona's constraint system applies at read time.

sense.description: How the persona perceives the world
sense.note: Model-level perception (vision, STT, document parsing) is declared via body.runtime.models, not as a sense faculty. Sense faculties are reserved for persona-level persistent perception behaviors distinct from model capabilities.

_gaps.emotionSensing: No safe external candidate — clawhub:emotion-state flagged Suspicious (data exfiltration risk). Local implementation path: pure prompt-layer inference, no external API, no data transmission. Target: layers/faculties/sense/emotion-sensing/.

skill.description: Discrete actions the persona can take on demand
skill.note: No skill is universally required — a conversational persona with zero skills is still a valid persona. find-skills + skill-vetter are a security pair: find-skills discovers capabilities, skill-vetter vets them before recommending installation — activating find-skills without skill-vetter creates a Trust Gradient gap. skill-creator is an independent optional for workflow codification. Built-in and scenario-specific skills are dormant until a preset activates them.

find-skills.author: vercel-labs
find-skills.security: Gen Agent Trust Hub Pass + Socket Pass + Snyk Warn — SnykWarn accepted per intakeCriteria.trustedSourceException: vercel-labs is a recognized organization (11.9K GitHub stars, 738K installs, no incident reports)
find-skills.note: On-demand capability discovery — when encountering a capability gap, persona searches the skills ecosystem and recommends 'npx skills add' to the user. Install decision stays with the user; persona does not self-install without consent. Security pair: must be activated together with skill-vetter — find-skills without skill-vetter leaves the Trust Gradient open at runtime.

skill-vetter.author: spclaudehome
skill-vetter.note: Security companion to find-skills — before recommending any skill installation, persona runs vetting protocol: source check, full code review for red flags (credential access, exfiltration patterns, obfuscation), permission scope, risk classification (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/EXTREME). Produces structured SKILL VETTING REPORT. Bridges persona-level discovery with OpenPersona's Install Gate trust model.

skill-creator.author: anthropics
skill-creator.security: Gen Agent Trust Hub Pass + Socket Pass + Snyk Pass — fully clean
skill-creator.note: On-demand skill synthesis — when user wants to codify a recurring workflow, persona follows skill-creator process to produce a reusable skill. Generated skill is reviewed and installed by user. Connects to P27 meta-cognition: metaCognitionLog patterns can trigger skill synthesis proposals.

_gaps.timeAwareness: No vetted external candidate (clawhub:time-awareness has 0 installs). Local implementation path: reads system clock, handles timezone, formats date/time for conversation context. Target: layers/skills/time-awareness/.

evolution.description: How the persona grows and changes over time
evolution.note: evolution.instance.boundaries is required by baseline policy, not by the framework — a persona without any evolution declaration is technically valid. Baseline mandates it to ensure all P11-grade personas have explicit growth constraints.

economy.note: Economy is opt-in. Companion/creative personas should NOT declare economy.enabled — financial concerns break immersion. Autonomous economic agents should enable it.

vitality.description: Multi-dimensional health score of the persona
vitality.note: Vitality pipeline (openpersona vitality score/report) is always available. Activation is on-demand — no persona.json field required.

_gaps.memoryHealth: No memory health dimension — cross-session memory quality, contradiction detection. Persona integrity depends on consistent memory; without this, evolvedTraits may contradict earlier stated preferences.
_gaps.relationshipHealth: No relationship health dimension — quality and progression of the user-persona relationship. Core to persona value proposition; relationship depth cannot be measured or surfaced without this.

social.description: Identity and presence in the agent ecosystem
social.note: agent-card.json and acn-config.json are auto-generated for all personas — no explicit persona.json field required to trigger generation.

rhythm.description: Temporal behavior — when and how often the persona acts
rhythm.note: Conservative defaults apply when rhythm is not declared. Proactive outreach (heartbeat) should be opt-in.

_meta.nextReview: After P11 ecosystem adaptation pilots — use pilot data to resolve _gaps and promote draft to v1.0

intakeCriteria.description: Applies to all external community skill sources (ClawHub, skills.sh, GitHub, etc.). First-party (acnlabs/*) and local (layers/) implementations are exempt.
rules: security: all audit checks Pass — any Fail = automatic reject; Warn from recognized organizations (vercel-labs, anthropics, microsoft, etc.) may be accepted with explicit note in sources entry (trustedSourceException); Warn from unknown authors = reject
market: ≥ 100 all-time installs — search score is irrelevant, only real install count counts
platform: no OS-specific dependencies for universal baseline — macOS-only = reject
license: MIT / Apache / MIT-0 / ISC or equivalent permissive license
identity: sources entry must include url + author + auditedVersion + lastVerifiedAt — slug alone is insufficient (slug re-registration and deletion risk)
intakeCriteria.rationale: Less is more — an empty sources block is honest; an unvetted reference creates false confidence. Architectural mismatch is NOT a reason to reject — if a skill passes all 4 criteria, the adoption pattern is soft-ref + Soul-layer bridge. OpenPersona provides the constraint system; the community skill provides the capability.
